pages.text: Everyone at Bright Stars School buzzed with excitement — the annual obstacle race was today! <name> tied his shoelaces tightly and took a deep breath. His friendly rival, Rohan, waved and said with a grin, 'Ready to lose, <name>?' 'Let’s see!' <name> laughed, feeling a mix of nerves and excitement.

The whistle blew and the race began! The first challenge was crawling under a long rope tunnel. <name> dropped to his knees and wriggled through like a quick little lizard. Rohan was fast too, crawling right beside him. They popped out almost together, giggling as grass stuck to their elbows.

Next came the jumping cones path. Colorful cones were lined up in zigzags. 'Boing! Boing! Boing!' <name> leaped carefully, imagining he was a frog hopping across lily pads. Rohan tried to jump too fast and toppled one cone. 'Oops!' Rohan chuckled, fixing it before moving on. <name> smiled — he liked that Rohan played fair.

Then came the trickiest part — the beanbag balance. Each racer had to put a beanbag on their head and walk across a wobbly wooden plank. <name> whispered, 'Steady… steady…' A sudden cheer from the crowd made him wobble, but he slowly regained balance. Rohan wobbled too and nearly dropped his beanbag. Without thinking, <name> gently held the plank to steady it for Rohan.

'Thanks,' Rohan said with a grateful smile. Now came the final obstacle — a short sack hop to the finish line! <name> and Rohan jumped into their sacks and hopped with all their might. The crowd clapped wildly. In the last few hops, <name> remembered a trick — small, quick jumps. He used it, and Rohan copied him. They bounced side by side, laughing.

With one final hop, <name> crossed the line just a tiny bit ahead. The playground burst into cheers and applause! Rohan patted <name>’s back. 'Great race! You earned it.' 'And you were awesome too!' <name> replied. Students lifted both boys’ hands like champions. Everyone enjoyed lemonade, clapped, and celebrated fair play, teamwork, and fun — the best victory of all.
